modules.title: DC Fundamentals
modules.description: Foundational principles of Direct Current circuits and electricity basics.

topics.title: Atomic Structure and Charge
learningObjectives: Explain the structure of an atom (protons, neutrons, electrons).
Define electrical charge and the unit of Coulomb.
Distinguish between conductors, insulators, and semiconductors.

topics.title: Voltage, Current, and Resistance
learningObjectives: Define Voltage as potential difference (Volts).
Define Current as the flow of electrons (Amperes).
Define Resistance as the opposition to flow (Ohms).
contentOutline: Factors affecting resistance (length, area, material, temperature)

topics.title: Ohm's Law and DC Power
learningObjectives: Apply Ohm's Law (V=IR) to solve circuit problems.
Calculate Power (Watts) in DC circuits using multiple formulas.
Understand the relationship between work, energy, and power.

topics.title: Series and Parallel Circuits
learningObjectives: Analyze characteristics of series circuits (current, voltage, resistance).
Analyze characteristics of parallel circuits (current, voltage, resistance).
Solve complex combination (series-parallel) circuits.

modules.title: AC Fundamentals
modules.description: Understanding Alternating Current, waveforms, and reactive components.

topics.title: AC Generation and Sine Waves
learningObjectives: Describe how AC is generated through induction.
Identify components of a sine wave (amplitude, period, frequency).
Calculate frequency and angular velocity.
contentOutline: Sine wave characteristics: Peak, RMS, Average

topics.title: Inductance, Capacitance, and Impedance
learningObjectives: Explain Inductive Reactance (XL) and its effect in AC.
Explain Capacitive Reactance (XC) and its effect in AC.
Calculate total Impedance (Z) in RLC circuits.

modules.title: Power (Single/Three-Phase)
modules.description: Analyzing power in single-phase and three-phase systems.

topics.title: The Power Triangle
learningObjectives: Differentiate between Real, Reactive, and Apparent power.
Calculate values using the Power Triangle (W, VAR, VA).

topics.title: Three-Phase Principles
learningObjectives: Explain the advantages of three-phase power over single-phase.
Calculate Line vs Phase voltages and currents in Wye and Delta.

modules.title: Transformers
modules.description: Principles, types, and calculations for electrical transformers.

topics.title: Mutual Induction and Ratios
learningObjectives: Explain how transformers transfer energy via mutual induction.
Calculate Turns, Voltage, and Current ratios.

topics.title: Types and Efficiency
learningObjectives: Identify common transformer types (Isolation, Auto, Step-up/down).
List common transformer losses and their causes.

modules.title: Electric Motors
modules.description: Operational theory of DC and AC motors.

topics.title: Motor Operating Principles
learningObjectives: Explain the interaction of magnetic fields to produce torque.
Identify major motor components.

topics.title: AC and DC Motor Types
learningObjectives: Compare Squirrel Cage vs Wound Rotor induction motors.
Explain characteristics of DC series, shunt, and compound motors.

modules.title: Power Factor
modules.description: Understanding and improving electrical system efficiency.

topics.title: Power Factor Theory
learningObjectives: Define Power Factor as the ratio of Real to Apparent power.
Identify causes of low power factor in industrial settings.
contentOutline: Impact on utility costs and equipment sizing

topics.title: Power Factor Correction
learningObjectives: Calculate the required KVAR to improve power factor.
Describe the use of capacitor banks for correction.

modules.title: Harmonics
modules.description: Dealing with non-linear loads and power quality issues.

topics.title: Sources of Harmonics
learningObjectives: Distinguish between linear and non-linear loads.
Identify common harmonic-generating equipment (VFDs, Rectifiers).

topics.title: Effects and Mitigation
learningObjectives: Explain the impact of triplen harmonics on neutral conductors.
Describe techniques for harmonic mitigation.

modules.title: Grounding and Bonding Theory
modules.description: Safety and protection through effective grounding systems.

topics.title: Grounding vs Bonding
learningObjectives: Define the difference between grounding (earth) and bonding (continuity).
Explain the purpose of an Effective Ground-Fault Current Path (EGFCP).

topics.title: Grounding Electrode Systems
learningObjectives: Identify common grounding electrodes (rods, plates, ufer).
Describe methods for testing earth resistance.
